description: When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. If the Aruba Central feature is enabled using this command, the switch will then reconnect back to Aruba Central and will get disconnected again. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.13.1170, what caveat is documented for Central?

Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. If the Aruba Central feature is enabled using this command, the switch will then reconnect back to Aruba Central and will get disconnected again. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.

description: When a hot-patch file download is triggered using the switch WebUI, log messages can incorrectly state that the file is added to the database with a missing status. This is a temporary state, and will correctly change to Not applied once the download is completed.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.13.1170, what caveat is documented for Hot Patch?

Feature Caveat: Hot Patch - When a hot-patch file download is triggered using the switch WebUI, log messages can incorrectly state that the file is added to the database with a missing status. This is a temporary state, and will correctly change to Not applied once the download is completed.

description: Pim Active-Active is not supported on overlay VXLAN SVIs.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.13.1170, what caveat is documented for PIM-SM?
Feature Caveat: PIM-SM - Pim Active-Active is not supported on overlay VXLAN SVIs.

description: When SNMP is enabled via the switch CLI, it can take between 1-2 minutes for the SNMP daemon to be ready to respond to requests. If a local or external SNMP MIB walk is performed in the interval between when SNMP is first enabled and the SNMP daemon is ready, the MIB walk action will return an error.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.13.1170, what caveat is documented for SNMP?

Feature Caveat: SNMP - When SNMP is enabled via the switch CLI, it can take between 1-2 minutes for the SNMP daemon to be ready to respond to requests. If a local or external SNMP MIB walk is performed in the interval between when SNMP is first enabled and the SNMP daemon is ready, the MIB walk action will return an error.

description: When a switch uses a certificate with a legacy certificate name that is not supported in 10.12 because it contains disallowed characters, the information will migrate properly in the upgrade, but that certificate can no longer be edited. For new certificate names, only alphanumeric characters, dots, dashes, and underscores are allowed.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.13.1170, what caveat is documented for Certificates?

Feature Caveat: Certificates - When a switch uses a certificate with a legacy certificate name that is not supported in 10.12 because it contains disallowed characters, the information will migrate properly in the upgrade, but that certificate can no longer be edited. For new certificate names, only alphanumeric characters, dots, dashes, and underscores are allowed.

description: For Classifier policy modifications to be secure, Aruba strongly encourages modifications be done as a three-step process: Bring down the port, modify, and bring the port back up.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.13.1170, what caveat is documented for Classifiers?

Feature Caveat: Classifiers - For Classifier policy modifications to be secure, Aruba strongly encourages modifications be done as a three-step process: Bring down the port, modify, and bring the port back up.

description: Policies containing both MAC and IPv6 classes are not allowed.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.13.1170, what limitation is mentioned for Classifiers?
Feature Caveat: Classifiers - Policies containing both MAC and IPv6 classes are not allowed.

description: No other checkpoint besides "startup-configuration" gets migrated during the upgrade process.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.13.1170, what caveat is documented for CMF?
Feature Caveat: CMF - No other checkpoint besides "startup-configuration" gets migrated during the upgrade process.

feature: DHCP Server, DHCP Relay, and DHCP Snooping
description: DHCP Relay and DHCP Snooping can co-exist on the same switch. DHCP Snooping and DHCP Server cannot co-exist on the same switch. DHCP Snooping, DHCP Relay, and DHCP Server together cannot co-exist on the same switch.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.13.1170, what caveat is documented for DHCP Server, DHCP Relay, and DHCP Snooping?

Feature Caveat: DHCP Server, DHCP Relay, and DHCP Snooping - DHCP Relay and DHCP Snooping can co-exist on the same switch. DHCP Snooping and DHCP Server cannot co-exist on the same switch. DHCP Snooping, DHCP Relay, and DHCP Server together cannot co-exist on the same switch.

description: Reserved ports or ports used by other applications/services within the system are not recommended to be used for other services. When two services use the same port there is chance of unexpected behaviors from these services. Best practice is to use unique port for each service across system.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.13.1170, what caveat is documented for IP-SLA?

Feature Caveat: IP-SLA - Reserved ports or ports used by other applications/services within the system are not recommended to be used for other services. When two services use the same port there is chance of unexpected behaviors from these services. Best practice is to use unique port for each service across system.

feature: IGMP/PIM on 6-in-6, Loopback and GRE interfaces
description: IGMP cannot be enabled on either Loopback or GRE interfaces. IGMP and PIM is not supported on a 6-in-6 Tunnel.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.13.1170, what caveat is documented for IGMP/PIM on 6-in-6, Loopback and GRE interfaces?
Feature Caveat: IGMP/PIM on 6-in-6, Loopback and GRE interfaces - IGMP cannot be enabled on either Loopback or GRE interfaces. IGMP and PIM is not supported on a 6-in-6 Tunnel.

description: Multicast traffic cannot be run under GRE, 6in4, or 6in6 tunnels.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.13.1170, what caveat is documented for Multicast?
Feature Caveat: Multicast - Multicast traffic cannot be run under GRE, 6in4, or 6in6 tunnels.

description: * VXLAN must be configured prior to configuring VSX. Multicast support for static VXLAN in the overlay has limited support. Contact Aruba Support for details.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.13.1170, what caveat is documented for Multicast and VXLAN?
Feature Caveat: Multicast and VXLAN - VXLAN must be configured prior to configuring VSX. Multicast support for static VXLAN in the overlay has limited support. Contact Aruba Support for details.

description: Priority-based flow control (PFC) is not supported on a split port.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.13.1170, what caveat is documented for PFC?
Feature Caveat: PFC - Priority-based flow control (PFC) is not supported on a split port.

description: REST supports the 'admin' and 'operator' roles but does not work with TACACS+ command authorization.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.13.1170, what caveat is documented for REST?
Feature Caveat: REST - REST supports the 'admin' and 'operator' roles but does not work with TACACS+ command authorization.

description: When configuring tunnels (VXLAN/IP tunnels) with the underlay as a static route, the next-hop IP should be an SVI or ROP IP and not configured as the Active-Gateway.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.13.1170, what caveat is documented for Tunnels?
Feature Caveat: Tunnels - When configuring tunnels (VXLAN/IP tunnels) with the underlay as a static route, the next-hop IP should be an SVI or ROP IP and not configured as the Active-Gateway.

description: Issuing the traceroute command with the ip-option loosesourceroute parameter fails inan overlay EVPN-VxLAN deployment.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.13.1170, what caveat is documented for Traceroute?
Feature Caveat: Traceroute - Issuing the traceroute command with the ip-option loosesourceroute parameter fails inan overlay EVPN-VxLAN deployment.

description: Traceroute v4/v6 over VXLAN fails to find intermediate next-hop IP information from a source VTEP in Virtual Active Gateway environment (the SVI is the same as theActive Gateway IP).

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.13.1170, what limitation is mentioned for Traceroute?

Feature Caveat: Traceroute - Traceroute v4/v6 over VXLAN fails to find intermediate next-hop IP information from a source VTEP in Virtual Active Gateway environment (the SVI is the same as theActive Gateway IP).

description: A GRE tunnel in an access-VLAN underlay is not supported in a non-default VRF.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.13.1170, what caveat is documented for GRE?
Feature Caveat: GRE - A GRE tunnel in an access-VLAN underlay is not supported in a non-default VRF.

description: Overlay/system partition information is included in HOST-RESOURCES-MIB polling. The overlay partition is a read-only partition and this is reflected in SNMP queries. hrFSAccess.3 = readOnly(2) is expected behavior for overlay partitions. Monitoring tools should not treat this as an error.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.13.1170, what limitation is mentioned for SNMP?

Feature Caveat: SNMP - Overlay/system partition information is included in HOST-RESOURCES-MIB polling. The overlay partition is a read-only partition and this is reflected in SNMP queries. hrFSAccess.3 = readOnly(2) is expected behavior for overlay partitions. Monitoring tools should not treat this as an error.

description: Symptom: MACs are only showing on one side of a VSX pair. For instance, a MAC is learned on a physical port on one VSX device and is missing from the peer VSX device. Scenario: This issue can occur resolving a network loop which causes the MACs to quickly move from one VSX device to the other, particularly when MACs are moving from EVPN tunnels to physical ports. Workaround: Using the CLI to clear the problematic MAC address.

workaround: Using the CLI to clear the problematic MAC address.

description: Symptom: ACL logging displays incorrect ACE sequence number and action while entries are being updated in hardware. Scenario: This issue occurs when an ACL entry is configured with the logging action the logged output may report an incorrect sequence number and action. Workaround: Wait for ACL programming to finish before utilizing logs for debug/analysis of traffic.
symptom: ACL logging displays incorrect ACE sequence number and action while entries are being updated in hardware.
scenario: This issue occurs when an ACL entry is configured with the logging action the logged output may report an incorrect sequence number and action.
workaround: Wait for ACL programming to finish before utilizing logs for debug/analysis of traffic.

description: Symptom: hpe-routing process crashes due to memory exhaustion during BGP neighbor flaps, Scenario: This issue may occur when high-frequency REST API monitoring (multiple calls per second) are combined with BGP peer reboot events.
symptom: hpe-routing process crashes due to memory exhaustion during BGP neighbor flaps,
scenario: This issue may occur when high-frequency REST API monitoring (multiple calls per second) are combined with BGP peer reboot events.

VSX (Bug ID 413525): Symptom: hpe-routing process crashes due to memory exhaustion during BGP neighbor flaps, Scenario: This issue may occur when high-frequency REST API monitoring (multiple calls per second) are combined with BGP peer reboot events.

description: Symptom: High CPU utilization from PTP daemon despite PTP being disabled, causing VSF reboots with Critical Service Fault. Scenario: This issue may occur after a system boot/restart, and the interface state changes on VSF stack members, leading to an agent crash/restart during the operation.
symptom: High CPU utilization from PTP daemon despite PTP being disabled, causing VSF reboots with Critical Service Fault.
scenario: This issue may occur after a system boot/restart, and the interface state changes on VSF stack members, leading to an agent crash/restart during the operation.

description: Symptom: OSPF API GET requests timeout when querying OSPF neighbors via REST API, displaying "maximum entries limit" error message. Scenario: This issue may occur when previous failed REST or CLI OSPF calls leave request ID uncleaned, causing subsequent API calls to hang.

description: Symptom: The neighbor <IP> graceful-shutdown command is accepted at the CLI but the configuration is not actually stored or shown in the running config. As a result the graceful-shutdown behavior is not available for the peer. Scenario: This issue occurs when the CLI accepts the command but show run bgp does not contain the neighbor <IP> graceful-shutdown line and the peer does not perform graceful-shutdown when expected.
symptom: The neighbor <IP> graceful-shutdown command is accepted at the CLI but the configuration is not actually stored or shown in the running config. As a result the graceful-shutdown behavior is not available for the peer.

description: Symptom: Switches allow configuring neighbor local-as in cases that constitute a “true” iBGP peer (where the neighbor’s remote-as matches the switch's configured global AS). Scenario: This issue occurs when a true iBGP peer is accepted by the switch as a local-as configuration.

description: Symptom: Classifier Policy is not enforced for traffic coming from an ISL link. Scenario: For a topology with VSX+ VRRP, if the policy is applied on SVI, all packets which reach the VRRP secondary device go to the primary device via the ISL. All of those packets are not getting classifier policy enforced. Even though the policy action is PBR redirect, those packets are not getting redirected. Workaround: Two workarounds are possible to prevent packets from being forwarded to VSX primary via ISL. 1. Use active gateway to give better active active redundancy. Packets reaching either VSX device process the packet destined to active gateway. 2. Turn off the interface connection from VRRP secondary device to down switch. This method may cause redundancy and is not preferred.

description: Symptom: STP ports are going into a Disabled/Blocking state. Scenario: This issue can occur after an edge device logs off and logs in back on a secured port or when a server connected to a VSX pair is rebooted. Workaround: Reboot the PVST process.
symptom: STP ports are going into a Disabled/Blocking state.
scenario: This issue can occur after an edge device logs off and logs in back on a secured port or when a server connected to a VSX pair is rebooted.
workaround: Reboot the PVST process.
